get_merge_request.title: Add faceted search to the catalog API
get_merge_request.description:
## What & why

Adds faceted filtering (brand, price range, availability) to `GET /catalog/search` so
the storefront can render filter sidebars without client-side filtering. Resolves the
slow-search complaints on large categories.

Jira: https://acme.atlassian.net/browse/CAT-512

## How to test

1. `npm install && npm run dev`
2. `GET /catalog/search?q=shoes&brand=acme&price=20-50`
3. Confirm the facets in the response match the applied filters.
4. Run `npm test -- catalog-search` to exercise the unit tests.

## Proof it was run

Response capture of a faceted query plus the passing test run:

```
GET /catalog/search?q=shoes&brand=acme -> 200, 18 results, 3 facets
PASS  src/catalog/search.test.ts (14 tests)
Test Suites: 1 passed, Tests: 14 passed
```

![faceted search response](https://acme.gitlab.io/uploads/facet-search.png)

## Notes for reviewer

Start in `src/catalog/search.ts` (facet aggregation); the route wiring in
`search.controller.ts` is thin. No breaking changes, no migrations.
get_merge_request.commit_summary: 4 commits: add facet aggregation, wire route, add tests, tidy

notes.body: Nice — the response capture and screenshot make this easy to verify.
